| MCP:iglpLp | # Spring Boot Template
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | A production-ready template for Spring Boot projects with modern best practices and built-in infrastructure.
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ![Docker Compose](https://imgur.com/qWLYDrg.png)
| MCP:iglpLp | ![Swagger UI](https://imgur.com/5MixfZe.png)
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Features
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Spring Boot 4.0.0** (Java 25)
| MCP:iglpLp | - **UUID Primary Keys** via Hibernate `@UuidGenerator`
| MCP:iglpLp | - **JWT Authentication & Authorization** with refresh tokens
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Role-based Security** with Spring Security
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Spring Boot Actuator** for health, info, and metrics endpoints
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Request Correlation ID** — automatic `X-Correlation-ID` header propagation and MDC logging
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Structured JSON Logging** — human-readable console + JSON file output (Logstash format)
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Pagination** — built into `BaseService` and controllers via Spring Data `Pageable`
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Rate Limiting** — annotation-based (`@RateLimit`, `@RateLimitCategory`) with configurable categories
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Idempotency** — `@Idempotent` annotation with Redis-backed request deduplication
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Swagger UI** for API documentation ([http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html))
| MCP:iglpLp | - **PostgreSQL**, **Redis**, **Kafka & Zookeeper** — all containerized via Docker Compose
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Multi-stage Dockerfile** with dependency caching and JRE runtime
| MCP:iglpLp | - **JaCoCo Code Coverage** reports
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Maven Enforcer** — ensures Java 25+ and Maven 3.8.7+
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Lombok** and **MapStruct** for reduced boilerplate
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Environment Isolation** — run dev, test, and prod in parallel via Docker Compose
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Unit & Integration Tests** for services, controllers, and infrastructure
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | > **Note:** Java 25 and Maven 3.8.7+ are required.
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ---
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Quick Start
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ### 1. Use This Template
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | Click **"Use this template"** on GitHub, then clone your new repository:
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ```sh
| MCP:iglpLp | git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/<your-repo>.git
| MCP:iglpLp | cd <your-repo>
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ### 2. Initialize Your Project
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | Run the initialization script to rename packages, modules, and containers to match your project:
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ```sh
| MCP:iglpLp | ./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact my-app --name "My App" --author "Your Name"
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | | Option | Description | Required |
| MCP:iglpLp | |--------|-------------|----------|
| MCP:iglpLp | | `--group-id` | Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g. `com.acme`) | Yes |
| MCP:iglpLp | | `--artifact` | Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. `my-app`) | Yes |
| MCP:iglpLp | | `--name` | Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact) | No |
| MCP:iglpLp | | `--author` | Author name for `@author` tags (default: `tzesh`) | No |
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | You can also run it interactively — omit the flags and you'll be prompted:
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ```sh
| MCP:iglpLp | ./init-project.sh
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | The script will:
| MCP:iglpLp | - Rename Java packages (`com.tzesh.springtemplate` -> your package)
| MCP:iglpLp | - Rename module directories (`spring-boot-template-api` -> `<artifact>-api`, etc.)
| MCP:iglpLp | - Update Docker container names, Kafka group IDs, and all references
| MCP:iglpLp | - Replace `@author` tags if `--author` is provided
| MCP:iglpLp | - Self-delete after completion
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ### 3. Verify the setup
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ```sh
| MCP:iglpLp | ./mvnw clean install
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ### 4. Start infrastructure and run
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ```sh
| MCP:iglpLp | # Start PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka
| MCP:iglpLp | docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | # Run the application
| MCP:iglpLp | ./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | Or run everything with Docker:
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ```sh
| MCP:iglpLp | docker compose up -d
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | The application will be available at [http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html).
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ---
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Project Structure
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | SpringBootTemplate/
| MCP:iglpLp | ├── pom.xml                          # Parent POM (enforcer, JaCoCo, shared deps)
| MCP:iglpLp | ├── spring-boot-template-core/       # Core module (base classes, annotations, exceptions)
| MCP:iglpLp | │   └── src/main/java/.../base/
| MCP:iglpLp | │       ├── annotation/              # @RateLimit, @Idempotent, etc.
| MCP:iglpLp | │       ├── dto/                     # BaseDTO
| MCP:iglpLp | │       ├── entity/                  # BaseEntity
| MCP:iglpLp | │       ├── exception/               # NotFoundException, RateLimitExceededException, etc.
| MCP:iglpLp | │       ├── handler/                 # @ControllerAdvice exception handlers
| MCP:iglpLp | │       ├── mapper/                  # BaseMapper (MapStruct)
| MCP:iglpLp | │       ├── response/                # BaseResponse wrapper
| MCP:iglpLp | │       └── service/                 # BaseService (CRUD + pagination)
| MCP:iglpLp | ├── spring-boot-template-api/        # API module (controllers, services, config)
| MCP:iglpLp | │   ├── Dockerfile                   # Multi-stage build
| MCP:iglpLp | │   └── src/main/java/.../
| MCP:iglpLp | │       ├── config/
| MCP:iglpLp | │       │   ├── correlation/         # CorrelationIdFilter
| MCP:iglpLp | │       │   ├── idempotency/         # IdempotencyAspect
| MCP:iglpLp | │       │   ├── ratelimit/           # RateLimitAspect, RateLimitingFilter
| MCP:iglpLp | │       │   └── security/            # SecurityChain, JWT filters
| MCP:iglpLp | │       ├── controller/              # REST controllers
| MCP:iglpLp | │       └── service/                 # Business services
| MCP:iglpLp | ├── docker-compose.yml               # PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, app
| MCP:iglpLp | ├── init-project.sh                  # Project initialization script
| MCP:iglpLp | ├── .editorconfig                    # Editor formatting rules
| MCP:iglpLp | ├── .gitattributes                   # Git line ending config
| MCP:iglpLp | └── .dockerignore                    # Docker build exclusions
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ---
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## API Usage
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | 1. **Register** a new user via `POST /auth/register`
| MCP:iglpLp | 2. **Login** via `POST /auth/login` to obtain a JWT token
| MCP:iglpLp | 3. **Authorize** in Swagger UI using the `Authorize` button
| MCP:iglpLp | 4. **Access** secured endpoints (e.g. user management requires `ADMIN` role)
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ---
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Actuator Endpoints
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | Health, info, and metrics are exposed at:
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | | Endpoint | Description |
| MCP:iglpLp | |----------|-------------|
| MCP:iglpLp | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/health` | Application health status |
| MCP:iglpLp | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/info` | Application info |
| MCP:iglpLp | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/metrics` | Application metrics |
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | Health details are shown when authenticated with an authorized role. Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes are enabled.
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ---
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Correlation ID
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | Every request is assigned a correlation ID for distributed tracing:
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | - If the client sends an `X-Correlation-ID` header, it is reused
| MCP:iglpLp | - Otherwise, a UUID is generated automatically
| MCP:iglpLp | - The correlation ID is added to the MDC for all log output and returned in the response header
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ---
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Rate Limiting
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ### Annotation-based (per-endpoint)
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ```java
| MCP:iglpLp | @RateLimit(limit = 20, duration = 1, key = RateLimitKeyStrategy.USER)
| MCP:iglpLp | @RateLimitCategory(RateLimitCategoryType.STANDARD)
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ### Configurable categories in `application.properties`
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | | Category | Default Limit | Duration |
| MCP:iglpLp | |----------|--------------|----------|
| MCP:iglpLp | | `strict` | 10 req | 1 min |
| MCP:iglpLp | | `standard` | 60 req | 1 min |
| MCP:iglpLp | | `relaxed` | 200 req | 1 min |
| MCP:iglpLp | | `authentication` | 5 req | 1 min |
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ---
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Idempotency
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | Annotate mutating endpoints with `@Idempotent` to prevent duplicate processing. Clients must send an `Idempotency-Key` header. Backed by Redis.
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ```java
| MCP:iglpLp | @PostMapping("/")
| MCP:iglpLp | @Idempotent
| MCP:iglpLp | public ResponseEntity<BaseResponse<UserDTO>> createUser(...) { ... }
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ---
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Kafka Messaging
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | - Kafka and Zookeeper are included in Docker Compose
| MCP:iglpLp | - `POST /kafka/send` sends messages to Kafka (supports optional scheduled delivery)
| MCP:iglpLp | - Consumer logs received messages
| MCP:iglpLp | - Logging level configurable in `application.properties`
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ---
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Environment Configuration
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | Use `.env` files for environment-specific variables and Docker Compose override files for port/resource separation:
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ```sh
| MCP:iglpLp | # Run test environment
| MCP:iglpLp | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | # Run prod environment
| MCP:iglpLp | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
| MCP:iglpLp | ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ---
| MCP:iglpLp |

| MCP:iglpLp | # Contributing to Spring Boot Template
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | Thank you for your interest in contributing! This guide will help you get started.
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Prerequisites
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Java 25** (Eclipse Temurin recommended)
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Maven 3.8.7+** (or use the included `./mvnw` wrapper)
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Docker & Docker Compose** (for running PostgreSQL, Redis, and Kafka locally)
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Setup
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | 1. **Clone the repository:**
| MCP:iglpLp |    ```bash
| MCP:iglpLp |    git clone https://github.com/Tzesh/SpringBootTemplate.git
| MCP:iglpLp |    cd SpringBootTemplate
| MCP:iglpLp |    ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | 2. **Start infrastructure services:**
| MCP:iglpLp |    ```bash
| MCP:iglpLp |    docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka
| MCP:iglpLp |    ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | 3. **Build the project:**
| MCP:iglpLp |    ```bash
| MCP:iglpLp |    ./mvnw clean install
| MCP:iglpLp |    ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | 4. **Run the application:**
| MCP:iglpLp |    ```bash
| MCP:iglpLp |    ./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
| MCP:iglpLp |    ```
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Code Style
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Indentation:** 4 spaces (no tabs)
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Line endings:** LF (Unix-style)
| MCP:iglpLp | - **Charset:** UTF-8
| MCP:iglpLp | - Use Lombok annotations where appropriate (`@RequiredArgsConstructor`, `@Getter`, etc.)
| MCP:iglpLp | - Follow existing patterns in `BaseService`, `BaseEntity`, and `BaseDTO`
| MCP:iglpLp | - Keep controllers thin — business logic belongs in services
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Branching
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | - `master` — stable, release-ready code
| MCP:iglpLp | - `feature/<name>` — new features
| MCP:iglpLp | - `fix/<name>` — bug fixes
| MCP:iglpLp | - `release-<version>` — release branches
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Pull Request Process
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | 1. Create a feature or fix branch from `master`
| MCP:iglpLp | 2. Make your changes with clear, focused commits
| MCP:iglpLp | 3. Ensure all tests pass: `./mvnw clean verify`
| MCP:iglpLp | 4. Open a PR against `master`
| MCP:iglpLp | 5. Provide a clear description of your changes
| MCP:iglpLp | 6. Wait for review and address feedback
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Testing
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | - Write unit tests for new services and controllers
| MCP:iglpLp | - Place tests in the corresponding `src/test/java` directory
| MCP:iglpLp | - Run tests: `./mvnw test`
| MCP:iglpLp | - Check code coverage reports in `target/site/jacoco/`
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | ## Questions?
| MCP:iglpLp | 
| MCP:iglpLp | Open an issue on GitHub if you have questions or need help.

data.content:
Based on my analysis of the repository, here's how environment-specific execution is documented in this template:

## Infrastructure Services with Docker Compose

The `docker-compose.yml` defines **5 services**:

| Service | Image | Purpose |
|---------|-------|---------|
| **db** | postgres:16 | PostgreSQL database |
| **redis** | redis:7 | Caching, sessions, rate limiting, idempotency |
| **zookeeper** | confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:7.5.0 | Kafka coordination |
| **kafka** | confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.5.0 | Event messaging |
| **app** | Custom Dockerfile | The Spring Boot application |

## Environment Isolation Strategy

The template uses a **three-layer configuration approach**:

### Layer 1: Docker Compose Environment Files
Four environment files define Docker/runtime variables:

| File | Profile | Database | User |
|------|---------|----------|------|
| `.env` / `.env.dev` | dev | spring_template_dev | development |
| `.env.test` | test | spring_template_test | test |
| `.env.qa` | qa | spring_template_qa | qa |
| `.env.prod` | prod | spring_template_prod | production |

### Layer 2: Spring Boot Profile Properties
Each environment gets a dedicated `application-{profile}.properties` with different configurations:

| Profile | Port | Log Level |
|---------|------|-----------|
| dev | 8080 | DEBUG |
| test | 8081 | INFO |
| qa | 8082 | INFO |
| prod | 8083 | WARN |

### Layer 3: Base Configuration
`application.properties` contains shared settings (JWT, rate limiting, Redis/Kafka connection details, actuator config).

## Running Different Environments

The README documents using Docker Compose override files for parallel execution:

```sh
# Test environment
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d

# Prod environment  
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
```

The `-p` flag creates separate Docker Compose projects, allowing **multiple environments to run simultaneously** without port/conflict issues.

## Test vs Production Distinction

- **Test/Dev environments**: Use PostgreSQL with DEBUG/INFO logging, lower rate limits for development
- **Production**: Uses WARN logging, production database credentials, potentially stricter configurations
- **Unit/Integration tests**: Use H2 in-memory database (not PostgreSQL), completely separate `src/test/resources/application.properties`

The override files (`docker-compose.test.override.yml`, `docker-compose.prod.override.yml`) are referenced but not present in the repository—they're expected to be created by users to customize port mappings and resource allocations per environment.
